brief: GAP/WHY: Radiofrequency balloon (RFB) ablation (HELIOSTAR, Biosense Webster; multi-electrode) developed to improve PV ablation efficiency vs point-by-point RF; unclear which RFB ablation parameters predict durable/chronic scar. DESIGN/SETTING: Experimental chronic canine animal model study; lesions verified against 3-month post-ablation late gadolinium enhancement cardiac MRI (LGE-CMR). POPULATION/N: 8 dogs (chronic canine model). INTERVENTION: Circumferential RFB ablation (RF energy to all electrodes simultaneously) at 4 sites: superior vena cava (SVC), right superior PV (RSPV), left inferior PV (LIPV), left atrial appendage (LAA); ablation duration 20 s or 60 s. Electroanatomic map ablation tags projected onto 3-month LGE-CMR; tags classified as ScarTags (correlated with CMR scar) vs Non-ScarTags (non-scar tissue). Effective parameters estimated by multivariate logistic regression. SAMPLE/LESIONS: 80 lesions SVC, 80 RSPV, 20 LIPV, 30 LAA; total 168 ScarTags + 42 Non-ScarTags (210 tags). KEY RESULTS (multivariate): (1) Electrode temperature before energy application associated with chronic scar — OR 0.805, p=0.0075 (lower pre-ablation temp favors scar). (2) Long RF duration associated with chronic scar — OR 2.360, p=0.0218. (3) Impedance drop NOT associated — OR 0.986, p=0.373. TAKEAWAY: Lower electrode temperature before ablation and longer ablation duration are the critical parameters for durable atrial scar formation with RFB ablation; impedance drop is not predictive.
